reasoning: The Lean signature's hypotheses — `P : Sylow p G` (p-Sylow subgroup), `hP : P.Normal` (P ⊲ G), and `hp : Nat.Prime p` (making explicit the implicit "p prime" behind "p-Sylow") — match the informal premises. The conclusion `Subgroup.Characteristic P` unfolds to `∀ ϕ : G ≃* G, P.comap ϕ.toMonoidHom = P`, which for bijective ϕ is equivalent to φ(P) = P for every automorphism φ of G — exactly the requested conclusion. So the Lean statement is logically equivalent to the informal claim.
